topic: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
researcher_bg: An HCI researcher specializing in Educational Technology (EdTech) and Human-AI Collaboration, focused on optimizing complex grading workflows and feedback quality.
expert_bg: A seasoned university instructor with extensive experience in evaluating lengthy project reports, currently managing high-volume grading pressure while prioritizing educational fairness and student feedback.

shared_root.label: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

exp_L1_00.label: Operational Realities of High-Volume Grading
exp_L1_00.description: The logistical and cognitive constraints of managing large stacks of lengthy reports under time pressure.

exp_L2_00_00.label: Session Management & Fatigue
exp_L2_00_00.description: How I structure grading sessions to maintain focus and avoid burnout.

exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: The Batching Strategy
exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: I never grade randomly; I chunk the stack into sets of 5 or 10 to keep my brain fresh, because switching contexts too often kills my consistency.

exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: The Mid-Stack Slump
exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: There's a specific point, usually 60% through the pile, where my eyes glaze over and I know I'm missing nuances just because I've read the same intro paragraph 40 times.

exp_L2_00_01.label: Standard Calibration
exp_L2_00_01.description: Methods used to establish and maintain consistent scoring standards across the grading period.

exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: The Anchor Paper Ritual
exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: I always grade 3 or 4 papers unofficially at the start to find a 'benchmark' paper that defines what a B+ looks like, so I have something concrete to compare against.

exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Grading Drift Sense
exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: It's a gut feeling that I'm getting stricter or looser as the night goes on, like I'm penalizing minor formatting errors more heavily just because I'm annoyed.

exp_L2_00_02.label: Time-Depth Trade-offs
exp_L2_00_02.description: Balancing the limited time available against the desire to provide meaningful evaluation.

exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: The Comment Budget
exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: I mentally allocate about 15 minutes per paper; if I spend 20 on one, I know I'm stealing time from the next student, so I have to stop writing comments somewhere.

exp_L1_01.label: Tacit Evaluative Judgments
exp_L1_01.description: The unwritten, experience-based heuristics used to assess quality beyond the explicit rubric.

exp_L2_01_00.label: Spotting Quality Signals
exp_L2_01_00.description: Immediate cues that indicate the overall level of student work before deep reading.

exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: The Executive Summary Tell
exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: I can usually guess the grade band just by reading the abstract; if they can't summarize their own work clearly there, the report is usually messy throughout.

exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Effort vs. Insight Balance
exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: I have to decide if a student worked really hard but missed the point, versus someone who nailed the concept with less flashiness; hard work doesn't always equal an A.

exp_leaf_01_00_02.label: The AI/Contract Cheating Smell
exp_leaf_01_00_02.description: Sometimes the writing style shifts abruptly or the tone is too perfect compared to their class participation, and I just suspect it without having hard proof.

exp_L2_01_01.label: Rubric Flexibility & Edge Cases
exp_L2_01_01.description: Handling student work that deviates from expectations or falls on grade boundaries.

exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: The Creative Pivot Dilemma
exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: When a student ignores the prompt structure but produces something brilliant, I struggle with whether to penalize them for not following instructions or reward the innovation.

exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Polish vs. Content Weight
exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: I try to ignore formatting, but if a report is visually painful to read, it subconsciously makes me assume the thinking is messy too, even if it's not.

exp_leaf_01_01_02.label: Borderline Energy Cost
exp_leaf_01_01_02.description: The papers right on the edge between a B and a C take twice as long to grade because I have to justify the decision to myself more than the clear A's or F's.

exp_L1_02.label: Pedagogical Impact & Fairness
exp_L1_02.description: Concerns regarding how grading decisions affect student learning, equity, and institutional accountability.

exp_L2_02_00.label: Feedback Utility
exp_L2_02_00.description: Ensuring that the comments provided actually help the student improve rather than just justifying the grade.

exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: The Read-Through Hope
exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: I spend time writing detailed comments, but I always worry students just look at the score and close the file, making my effort wasted.

exp_leaf_02_00_01.label: Actionable Specificity
exp_leaf_02_00_01.description: I try to avoid writing 'good job' or 'vague'; I force myself to write comments that tell them exactly what to change next time, which takes more mental energy.

exp_L2_02_01.label: Fairness & Accountability
exp_L2_02_01.description: Anxieties about equity, bias, and defending grading decisions to students or administration.

exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: The Order Effect Worry
exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: I worry that the first few papers get graded harder because I'm setting the standard, or the last few get rushed, creating an unfair advantage based on submission order.

exp_leaf_02_01_01.label: Contextual Equity Knowledge
exp_leaf_02_01_01.description: I sometimes know a student is going through personal issues, and I have to decide internally if I accommodate them informally or stick strictly to the rubric for fairness to others.

exp_leaf_02_01_02.label: The Defensible Grade Mindset
exp_leaf_02_01_02.description: I write certain comments not for the student, but to protect myself if they appeal the grade; I need a paper trail that shows I followed the rubric.

shared_root.label: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
shared_root.description: Interview topic: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

res_L1_00.label: Grading Workflow & Cognitive Dynamics
res_L1_00.description: Examining the procedural steps and mental effort required during the evaluation process.

res_L2_00_00.label: Process Mapping
res_L2_00_00.description: Understanding the sequence and structure of grading tasks.

res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Map end-to-end grading workflow
res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Document the sequential steps educators take from receiving submissions to finalizing grades.

res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Identify high-friction steps
res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Pinpoint specific stages in the workflow that cause delays or frustration.

res_leaf_00_00_02.label: Analyze context switching costs
res_leaf_00_00_02.description: Evaluate the mental effort lost when switching between different student submissions or tasks.

res_L2_00_01.label: Cognitive Load
res_L2_00_01.description: Assessing the mental and emotional strain during evaluation.

res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Pinpoint exhaustion phases
res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Identify which specific phase of grading causes the most cognitive or emotional fatigue.

res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Assess volume impact
res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Understand how massive volumes of assignments affect mindset and performance at term end.

res_leaf_00_01_02.label: Evaluate emotional toll
res_leaf_00_01_02.description: Measure the emotional exhaustion associated with repetitive evaluation tasks.

res_L1_01.label: Evaluation Logic & Fairness
res_L1_01.description: Investigating how experts make judgments and maintain consistency.

res_L2_01_00.label: Decision Making
res_L2_01_00.description: Exploring how educators handle standard and non-standard submissions.

res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Rubric recalibration
res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Explore how experts internally weigh and adjust standards when work falls outside predefined rubrics.

res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Intuition articulation
res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Extract the objective cues that underlie an expert's 'gut feeling' about quality.

res_leaf_01_00_02.label: Heuristic patterns
res_leaf_01_00_02.description: Identify rapid gauging patterns used to distinguish between grade levels (e.g., A vs C).

res_L2_01_01.label: Consistency & Bias
res_L2_01_01.description: Understanding concerns regarding fairness and grading drift.

res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Grading drift awareness
res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Measure educator awareness of shifting standards during prolonged evaluation sessions.

res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Fairness anxiety
res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Understand feelings of anxiety regarding educational fairness when standards shift due to fatigue.

res_leaf_01_01_02.label: Consistency triggers
res_leaf_01_01_02.description: Identify what prompts an educator to pause and recalibrate their grading standards.

res_L1_02.label: AI Collaboration & Feedback
res_L1_02.description: Defining the role of AI in enhancing feedback and automating workflows.

res_L2_02_00.label: Feedback Quality
res_L2_02_00.description: Defining what constitutes valuable feedback for students.

res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Excellent vs Passing
res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Define the fundamental differences between high-quality and merely sufficient feedback comments.

res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Student reaction
res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Anticipate the specific desired reaction from students when crafting personalized feedback.

res_leaf_02_00_02.label: Human element
res_leaf_02_00_02.description: Determine which essential human elements must remain in feedback comments versus automated text.

res_L2_02_01.label: Automation Boundaries
res_L2_02_01.description: Identifying opportunities and limits for AI assistance.

res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Elimination candidates
res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Identify specific workflow steps experts would eliminate without compromising educational value.

res_leaf_02_01_01.label: Trust requirements
res_leaf_02_01_01.description: Assess the level of trust required for educators to accept AI-assisted grading suggestions.

res_leaf_02_01_02.label: Oversight needs
res_leaf_02_01_02.description: Define boundaries where human oversight remains mandatory despite AI capabilities.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

structural_mapping.inputs: Abstract text vs. Workflow step data
structural_mapping.logic: Quality heuristic vs. Automation value heuristic
structural_mapping.outputs: Skip deep read vs. Skip human step

analogy.explanation: Just as you skip deep reading when the abstract signals low quality, the tool skips human steps when signals show low educational value.

assistance_payload.scenario: You spot a sudden tone shift in a report, signaling potential contract cheating to your intuition. The system marks this verification step as eliminable because previous rubric scores were consistent. Removing this check saves time but deletes your only chance to catch the integrity issue early.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: Your focus on navigating the tension between rewarding student innovation and enforcing prompt structure is highly applicable to real-world grading.

pivot.limitation: but we might encounter hidden inconsistencies where mental fatigue subtly shifts your threshold for accepting these creative pivots
pivot.research_goal: understanding how fairness anxiety impacts evaluative consistency
pivot.compelling_reason: Solving this helps us overcome the limitation because it ensures your intuitive judgments remain defensible and stable even during high-volume grading periods.
pivot.coarse_scenario: Imagine finishing a long grading session where you confidently flag a brilliant rule-breaker without second-guessing yourself, knowing your consistency holds up even when you're tired. You walk away knowing every student received the same quality of judgment, regardless of when their paper was read.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

probes.question: Is your negative reaction triggered by dense text blocks or inconsistent spacing?
Is the friction caused by missing table of contents or inconsistent heading styles?
Is the lack of polish due to informal language or missing branding elements?
Do mixed fonts bother you more than inconsistent font sizes?
Is the difficulty caused by complex sentence structures or lack of white space?
Do spelling errors reduce trust more than alignment issues?
Is the confusion caused by missing section headers or unnumbered lists?
Does shifting between formal and casual tone impact quality perception more than vocabulary choice?
choices: Shifting between formal and casual tone

hypothetical_scenarios.scenario: If text density increased by 50% but content remained identical, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If hyperlinks were removed from a digital report but headings remained, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If a company logo was removed from the header but layout stayed perfect, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If the font family switched randomly every page but readability stayed high, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If paragraph length doubled without adding new information, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If three typos appeared in a 50-page document versus none, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If bolding was removed from all section titles but text order stayed same, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?
If one section used slang while the rest was formal, would your judgment about 'Polish vs. Content Weight' change?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

probes.question: Is the grading hesitation caused by vague wording in the rubric or conflicting interpretations of specific terms?
Is the uncertainty due to a lack of supporting data or the presence of contradictory data?
Does the delay stem from recalling specific rules or calculating the weighted impact on the final grade?
choices: Calculating the weighted impact on the final grade

probes.question: Is the difficulty driven by poor writing structure or incorrect technical terminology?
Is the borderline status due to missing a minor section or missing a core argument?
Are you comparing this paper to the average of the stack or the specific paper graded immediately before?
Is the hesitation based on the objective quality or the likelihood of the student requesting a regrade?
choices: Likelihood of the student requesting a regrade

probes.question: Is the decision weighted more on procedural accuracy or conceptual understanding?

hypothetical_scenarios.scenario: If the rubric wording changed from "demonstrates understanding" to "lists three examples", would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If the student provided two additional citations supporting their claim, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If you had a checklist automation tool to calculate weighted scores, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If the paper contained perfect grammar but incorrect concepts, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If the conclusion section was entirely omitted but the analysis was sound, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If this paper was the first one you graded instead of the tenth, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If the student had a history of attending office hours, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?
If the paper missed minor details but nailed the core thesis, would your judgment about 'Borderline Energy Cost' change?

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

assistance_payload.validate_focus: Your focus on ensuring students actually read and value your detailed comments is highly applicable, as feedback is useless if it remains unseen.

pivot.limitation: However, we might encounter a bottleneck where hesitation to rely on AI limits the consistency and timeliness of that feedback.
pivot.research_goal: Solving the requirement for educator trust in AI suggestions
pivot.compelling_reason: helps us overcome this because it ensures the tool becomes a reliable partner that frees up your energy to personalize the comments students actually value.
pivot.coarse_scenario: In a typical week after adoption, you confidently approve AI-drafted insights early in the process. This leaves you ample time to add the human touch that convinces students to open the file and engage deeply with your guidance.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

assistance_payload.vulnerable_assumption: That there are specific workflow steps in the feedback process that can be eliminated or automated without compromising educational value.
assistance_payload.domain_correction: Experts often view high-cognitive-load tasks, such as crafting actionable specific feedback, as essential to educational quality and not suitable for elimination.
assistance_payload.safe_phrasing: How do you determine which parts of the grading workflow require your personal cognitive effort versus those that might be open to tool support or streamlining?
assistance_payload.misalignment_reason: Expert views specific feedback as a necessary effort, while Researcher investigates which steps can be eliminated/automated.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

structural_mapping.inputs: Student circumstances correspond to previous task residue
structural_mapping.logic: Accommodation decisions correspond to mental reset efforts
structural_mapping.outputs: Informal fairness adjustments correspond to consistency drift

analogy.explanation: Just as knowing a student's struggle silently alters your fairness calibration, switching submissions silently consumes mental energy that alters your consistency calibration.

assistance_payload.scenario: You grade a complex project immediately after a simple essay, feeling your brain stumble to recalibrate expectations. My tool measures that stumble as wasted time rather than a positional bias affecting the score itself. A late submission gets penalized for your mental fatigue from switching topics, not its actual place in line.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

structural_mapping.inputs: Student submission plus external pressure (appeal risk vs. automation limits)
structural_mapping.logic: Filtering comments to satisfy a secondary constraint (legal protection vs. human authenticity)
structural_mapping.outputs: Text serving dual purposes (instruction + proof vs. guidance + efficiency)

analogy.explanation: Just as you craft comments to satisfy an auditor rather than just the student, the researcher categorizes feedback to satisfy system constraints versus human connection. Both involve generating text where the visible output serves a hidden secondary purpose beyond pure assessment.

assistance_payload.scenario: You notice a student's illness and silently lower expectations during grading. The AI tool instead tags which feedback sentences sound empathetic versus automated for transparency. Your private accommodation stays invisible, but the tool forces every human nuance into the visible comment log.

aligned_path_expert: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.
aligned_path_researcher: Exploring challenges, cognitive load, and expert intuition in grading project-based assignments to inform the design of AI-assisted evaluation tools.

structural_mapping.logic: Monitoring for drift from intended standard

analogy.explanation: Just as you monitor submission order to prevent drift in harshness, consistency triggers monitor decision patterns to prevent drift in rubric interpretation.

assistance_payload.scenario: You document rubric violations specifically to withstand a formal grade appeal later. My tool flags moments where your scoring deviates from your earlier batches to prompt recalibration. Conflict arises when your defensive documentation justifies a score that my system identifies as a statistical inconsistency requiring adjustment.
